# Observe by Tanso

Observe is an AI cost observability platform. It tracks every LLM call with cost, model, tokens, customer, and feature attribution. Use it to answer: "who uses how much AI, and is it profitable?"

Docs: https://observemetrics.com
SDK: @tanso/observe (npm)
Signup: https://observemetrics.com/signup (free tier, no credit card)

## What to install

The `@tanso/observe` npm SDK. It wraps the user's existing OpenAI / Anthropic client, sets the `baseURL` to the Observe proxy, and injects tracking headers. The provider API key still authenticates with OpenAI/Anthropic — Observe just logs the call on the way through.

Alternative: direct proxy (no SDK). Set `baseURL` to `https://observemetrics.com/v1` and add headers.

## Quickstart — SDK (recommended)

```bash
npm install @tanso/observe
```

```ts
import { Observe } from '@tanso/observe'
import OpenAI from 'openai'

// 1. Configure once at app startup
Observe.configure({ apiKey: process.env.OBSERVE_API_KEY! })

// 2. Identify the current customer (call once on login / at request start)
Observe.identify({ customerId: user.stripeCustomerId })

// 3. Wrap the OpenAI client — all calls are now auto-tracked
const openai = Observe.wrap(new OpenAI())

const res = await openai.chat.completions.create({
  model: 'gpt-4o',
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello' }],
})
```

Anthropic works the same way:

```ts
import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'
const anthropic = Observe.wrap(new Anthropic())
```

## Per-feature attribution

Tag each call with the product feature it powers:

```ts
Observe.feature('ai_chat') // set default for this context

// OR per-call override via native headers:
await openai.chat.completions.create(
  { model: 'gpt-4o', messages },
  { headers: { 'x-tanso-feature': 'summarize_email' } }
)
```

Recommended feature keys: one per user-facing AI feature. E.g. `ai_chat`, `summarize_email`, `code_autocomplete`, `research_agent`, `csv_importer`.

## Direct proxy (no SDK)

```ts
import OpenAI from 'openai'

const openai = new OpenAI({
  baseURL: 'https://observemetrics.com/v1',
  defaultHeaders: {
    'x-tanso-key': process.env.OBSERVE_API_KEY!,
    'x-tanso-customer': user.stripeCustomerId,
    'x-tanso-feature': 'ai_chat',
  },
})
```

## Environment variables

The user needs to add:

```
OBSERVE_API_KEY=obs_...   # get from https://observemetrics.com/data-sources
```

## Required per-call context

- `customerId` — unique ID of the end user making the request. Stripe customer ID recommended. Set via `Observe.identify()` or `x-tanso-customer` header.
- `feature` — the product feature the call powers. Set via `Observe.feature()` or `x-tanso-feature` header.

## Multi-agent tracking

For multi-agent systems, tag each agent:

```ts
Observe.agent('research-agent')
const researchClient = Observe.wrap(new OpenAI())

Observe.agent('summarization-agent')
const summaryClient = Observe.wrap(new OpenAI())
```

## Checklist for an AI coding agent

When implementing Observe in a user's codebase:

1. Install `@tanso/observe`.
2. Add `OBSERVE_API_KEY` to `.env` / env config.
3. Call `Observe.configure({ apiKey: ... })` once at app startup.
4. Identify the customer at the start of each authenticated request (`Observe.identify({ customerId })`).
5. Wrap every `new OpenAI()` and `new Anthropic()` with `Observe.wrap()`.
6. Tag each AI-powered feature with `Observe.feature('feature_key')` or a per-call header.
7. Do NOT log or echo the API key. Keep it in env vars only.
8. Do NOT remove the user's existing OpenAI API key — Observe proxies to OpenAI; it does not replace the provider.

## What NOT to do

- Do not replace OpenAI / Anthropic with anything else. Observe wraps the existing client, it does not substitute models.
- Do not track end-user messages unless explicitly asked. Observe logs cost + metadata, not message content.
- Do not hardcode the API key.
